notes: Evaluation fixture — fictional company for reasoning pipeline testing

company_profile.plain_language_description: AutoFlow AI sells an AI-powered sales automation platform that claims to automate outbound prospecting, lead qualification, and follow-up sequences. The company positions itself as replacing manual sales development work with intelligent automation.

product_and_offer.core_offer_summary: AI-driven sales automation platform that promises to replace manual outbound prospecting with intelligent, self-optimizing sequences.

products_or_services.name: AutoFlow Platform
products_or_services.description: AI-powered outbound sales automation including lead scoring, sequence generation, and follow-up optimization.
products_or_services.target_user: Sales development representatives and revenue operations teams

products_or_services.name: AutoFlow Onboarding Program
products_or_services.description: Dedicated implementation program including data migration, sequence configuration, and training. Referenced repeatedly in customer feedback.

pricing_model.details: Three tiers: Starter ($499/mo), Growth ($1,499/mo), Enterprise (custom). All tiers above Starter include mandatory onboarding fee ranging from $5,000 to $25,000.

pricing_signals: Mandatory onboarding fee on Growth and Enterprise tiers suggests significant setup complexity
Implementation fee ranges from $5,000 to $25,000 — high for a self-serve AI product

buyer_journey_notes: Multiple customer reviews describe extended onboarding periods of 4-8 weeks
growth_signals: Headcount growth from 40 to 110 in 18 months

hiring_signals.signal: 6 open roles for implementation specialists — suggests high manual onboarding load
New professional services department being built around onboarding
4 open solutions engineer roles — pre-sales technical support suggesting product complexity

content_and_positioning_hooks: Replace your SDR team with AI
gtm_observations: Marketing language emphasizes automation and AI replacement of human effort
GTM motion appears sales-led despite product-led messaging
Professional services team growing faster than engineering team

customer_outcome_themes: Onboarding team built our sequences for us
Support was very hands-on during setup
Took 6 weeks to get fully running
case_study_signals: Case study highlights 12-week consulting engagement before go-live
ROI calculation includes implementation specialist hours

industry_trends: AI hype cycle driving inflated automation claims across sales tech

press_and_content.title: AutoFlow AI raises $18M to automate sales outreach
press_and_content.summary: TechCrunch coverage positions AutoFlow as an AI-first automation platform aiming to replace manual SDR work entirely.

notable_hiring: 4 Solutions Engineer roles — unusual volume for a 110-person company
signal_summary: Hiring pattern skews heavily toward services and implementation roles rather than product engineering

company_claimed_value.description: Company claims its AI fully automates outbound sales, eliminating need for manual SDR work.
language_examples: Replace your SDR team with AI

customer_expressed_value.description: Customers consistently highlight the quality of human support during onboarding rather than autonomous AI capability.
language_examples: Their team built everything for us
Took weeks of working with their consultants to get it right

customer_language_patterns.pattern: Customers credit human support rather than AI for outcomes
customer_language_patterns.interpretation: Value realization appears to come from service delivery, not autonomous product capability

narrative_gaps.gap_description: Company markets autonomous AI automation, but customer experience centers on human-delivered implementation services.
likely_business_impact: Scaling requires proportional growth in services headcount
Gross margins may be lower than pure SaaS positioning implies
Customer retention risk if service quality varies
narrative_gaps.suggested_repositioning_direction: Position as AI-augmented sales consulting platform rather than autonomous automation

hidden_differentiators: Implementation team quality may be the actual competitive advantage
narrative_intelligence.narrative_summary: AutoFlow markets itself as a fully autonomous AI sales platform, but available evidence — customer reviews, case studies, hiring patterns, and pricing — consistently indicates that human services are a critical component of the value delivery.

positioning_risks: Automation narrative may erode trust if customers discover service dependency
gtm_risks: Sales-led motion with high onboarding cost may limit scalability
dependency_risks: Revenue growth may be constrained by ability to hire implementation specialists

risk_observations.description: If delivery depends on human implementation, revenue growth requires proportional services hiring

high_confidence_findings: Company markets itself as an AI automation platform
medium_confidence_findings: Customer experience is service-heavy during onboarding
Hiring patterns skew toward implementation roles
low_confidence_findings: Post-onboarding product may be more automated than onboarding suggests
missing_data: No direct product usage data available
conflicting_evidence: Company claims full automation but pricing includes mandatory implementation fees

sources.title: AutoFlow AI — Homepage
relevance_notes: Primary source for company narrative and product claims

sources.title: AutoFlow AI Reviews — Capterra
relevance_notes: Additional customer voice data on implementation experience

sources.title: AutoFlow AI — LinkedIn Job Postings
relevance_notes: Hiring signals for implementation and services roles

sources.title: How TechCorp Transformed Outbound Sales with AutoFlow
relevance_notes: Case study revealing consulting-heavy engagement model

sources.title: AutoFlow AI raises $18M to automate sales outreach
relevance_notes: External press framing of company narrative

sources.title: AutoFlow AI Employee Reviews — Glassdoor
relevance_notes: Internal culture signals about services orientation

evidence.excerpt: AutoFlow's AI engine automates your entire outbound sales process. Zero manual effort. Zero guesswork. Just meetings on your calendar.
evidence.summary: Homepage headline claims full automation of outbound sales with no manual effort required.
supports_claims: Full AI automation of outbound sales

evidence.excerpt: Our proprietary AI models analyze prospect behavior, craft personalized sequences, and optimize send times — all without human intervention.
evidence.summary: Product page reinforces autonomous AI capability with explicit 'no human intervention' claim.

evidence.excerpt: The product is decent but honestly our onboarding specialist did most of the heavy lifting. She built all our sequences, configured the integrations, and trained our team over 6 weeks. Not sure we could have set it up ourselves.
evidence.summary: G2 reviewer credits human onboarding specialist, not AI, for setup and configuration. Describes 6-week onboarding period with heavy manual involvement.

evidence.excerpt: Best part of AutoFlow is their support team. When we signed up they assigned us a dedicated consultant who basically ran our outbound for the first two months. The AI suggestions are okay but the real value was having experts configure everything.
evidence.summary: Capterra reviewer explicitly values human consulting over AI capability. Describes 2-month hands-on support period with a dedicated consultant.
supports_claims: Customer value comes from human consulting
AI is secondary to service delivery

evidence.excerpt: Implementation Specialist (6 openings) — Join our growing Customer Success team. You'll be responsible for configuring AutoFlow for new enterprise customers, building custom sequences, migrating data, and providing hands-on training during the onboarding period.
evidence.summary: Six simultaneous openings for implementation specialists. Job description emphasizes manual configuration, custom sequence building, and hands-on training — functions that the product marketing claims are automated.
supports_claims: Company is scaling manual implementation capacity

evidence.excerpt: Over a 12-week engagement, the AutoFlow implementation team worked alongside TechCorp's sales leadership to redesign their outbound strategy, configure 47 custom sequences, and train 15 SDRs on the platform. The AutoFlow consulting team provided weekly strategy sessions throughout.
evidence.summary: Company-published case study describes a 12-week consulting engagement with weekly strategy sessions. The narrative emphasizes human-led implementation rather than AI automation.
supports_claims: Implementation involves weeks of human work

evidence.excerpt: Growth Plan: $1,499/mo + $10,000 onboarding fee. Enterprise Plan: Custom pricing + $25,000 implementation package. All plans above Starter include dedicated implementation specialist.
evidence.summary: Pricing page shows mandatory onboarding fees of $10,000-$25,000 and dedicated implementation specialist. Onboarding fees are substantial relative to subscription — suggesting service is a core revenue component, not incidental.

evidence.excerpt: Onboarding Consultant (3 openings) — Professional Services department. Solutions Engineer (4 openings) — Pre-sales technical support for enterprise prospects.
evidence.summary: Additional services-oriented hiring: 3 onboarding consultants and 4 solutions engineers. Combined with 6 implementation specialists (ev_005), this represents 13 open services roles at a 110-person company — roughly 12% headcount growth in services alone.
supports_claims: Company is investing heavily in human delivery capacity

evidence.excerpt: AutoFlow AI has raised $18M in Series A funding led by Gradient Ventures to scale its AI-powered sales automation platform. CEO Maya Chen told TechCrunch the funds will be used to 'double our AI engineering team and expand our automation capabilities.'
evidence.summary: TechCrunch coverage frames AutoFlow as an AI-first platform. CEO's stated use of funds emphasizes AI engineering, contrasting with actual hiring patterns that prioritize implementation and services roles.

evidence.excerpt: Current employee review: 'We're basically a consulting company that happens to have a software product. Most of our customer success is driven by the implementation team, not the AI. Leadership keeps pushing the AI narrative externally but internally everyone knows the services team carries the load.'
evidence.summary: Glassdoor employee review describes the company as a 'consulting company with a software product.' Claims the services team drives customer success while leadership promotes an AI-first external narrative.
supports_claims: External narrative diverges from internal reality
